lines.en_lit: provided that he does not skip from the end of the book to its beginning,
furls the Torah scroll And why all this? so as not to cast aspersions on the Torah scroll,

“And on the tenth,” from the book of Numbers he reads by heart. Why Let him furl and read it Rav Huna, son of Rav Yehoshua, said Rav Sheshet said: because one may not furl a Torah scroll in public, out of respect for the community.
let another be brought and read Rav Huna bar Yehuda said: due to a flaw in the first And Reish Lakish said due to unnecessary blessing
Are we concerned a flaw? Didn’t Rabbi Yitzḥak Nappaḥa say: the New Moon of Tevet, occurs on Shabbat, one brings three Torah One reads one the topic of the day, and one of the New Moon; and one
three men from three scrolls there is no a flaw, one man from two scrolls, there is a flaw,

And recites eight blessings The Sages taught concerning the Torah in the way one recites a blessing in the synagogue: concerning the service, concerning thanksgiving, and concerning pardon of iniquity, according to their established forms concerning the Temple in and of itself; concerning the priests in and of themselves; concerning the Jewish people in and of itself; concerning the rest of the prayer.

The Sages taught And concerning the rest of the prayer Song, supplication, petition before You for Your people Israel, who need to be saved. And he concludes with: The One Who hears prayer. And after each and every brings a Torah scroll from his house, and reads from it in order to show its appearance to the community.
One who sees the High Priest not because one is not permitted obvious lest you say that in accordance with Reish Lakish, as Reish Lakish said: One does not pass over mitzvot,
And what mitzva “The king’s glory is in the multitude of people” teaches us

MISHNA: If read in fine linen garments, he sanctified his hands and feet removed descended and immersed. ascended and dried himself and they brought him golden garments and he dressed and sanctified his hands and feet.

He emerged and offered his ram and the ram of the people and the seven unblemished year-old lambs the statement of Rabbi Eliezer. Rabbi Akiva says: rather, with the daily morning offering; and the bull burnt-offering; and the goat whose are performed outside were sacrificed with the daily afternoon offering.
he sanctified his hands and feet and removed and he descended and immersed and ascended and dried himself.

They brought him the white garments and he dressed and sanctified his hands and feet. he entered to take out the spoon and the coal pan, He sanctified his hands and feet and removed and descended and immersed and ascended and dried himself

They brought him golden garments, and he dressed and sanctified his hands and feet and entered to burn the afternoon incense and to remove the lamps, And he sanctified his hands and feet and removed and he descended and immersed and ascended and dried himself.
They brought him his own clothing and he dressed, escort him to his house And would make a feast for his loved ones when he emerged in peace from the Sanctuary.

GEMARA: A dilemma was raised before What is saying? were sacrificed with the daily morning offering, the bull burnt-offering and the goat whose are performed outside were sacrificed with the daily afternoon offering?

Or perhaps this is what he is saying: were sacrificed with the daily morning offering the bull burnt-offering together with them, the goat whose are performed outside, with the daily afternoon offering.
And furthermore, the bull burnt-offering, to Rabbi Eliezer’s he omitted it, when performs
And furthermore, both Rabbi Eliezer’s and Rabbi Akiva’s portions of the sin-offering when performs their
Rava said: You will only find it properly to Rabbi Eliezer as taught by the school Shmuel, or to Rabbi Akiva as in the Tosefta.

The school of Shmuel taught Rabbi Eliezer says: He came out and offered his ram and the ram of the people and the portions of the sin-offering But the bull burnt-offering and the seven lambs and the goat whose are performed outside with the daily afternoon offering.

What is Rabbi Akiva’s Tosefta? As it was taught Rabbi Akiva says: The bull burnt-offering and the seven lambs were sacrificed with the daily morning offering, as it is stated “Besides the morning burnt-offering which is the daily burnt-offering And afterward the service of the day,
